_comment: THE σ-CHAIN — The Divisor Sum Walk Through E8
The divisor sum function σ(n) adds up ALL positive divisors
of n, including 1 and n itself.
Starting from 28 (the number of rotation planes in 8D),
iterating σ produces a chain that visits every fundamental
structure in E8. This is not cherry-picked — each value
has a known, independent meaning in the framework.
The chain also connects to a PARALLEL chain starting from
60 (the order of A₅, the icosahedral group). Both chains
merge at 120, demonstrating that the σ function naturally
weaves together different branches of mathematics.

divisor_sum_function.definition: σ(n) = sum of all positive divisors of n (including 1 and n)

primary_chain._comment: Starting from the number of rotation planes in 8D

steps.meaning: C(8,2) = number of independent rotation planes in 8D
Population of D8 (integer-type) shells in E8

steps.also: 5! = 120 vertices of the 600-cell; dimension of Level 2

steps.meaning: |A₆| = order of alternating group on 6 elements

secondary_chain._comment: Starting from the icosahedral group order

merger_point._comment: Both chains share the value 120, demonstrating convergence

framework_primes._comment: Five primes that appear repeatedly throughout the framework
